headlines.title: Detroit-bound flight diverted to Canada after passenger from Congo boarded ‘in error’ amid Ebola outbreak
headlines.description: An Air France flight bound for Detroit was redirected to Canada after a passenger from Congo boarded “in error” amid the Ebola outbreak in central Africa, officials said.

headlines.title: American doctor with Ebola flown to Germany after operating on patient with deadly virus
headlines.description: By the time the American surgeon who contracted Ebola was flown to Germany, he was barely able to stand on his own, according to leaders of the Christian missionary group with which he worked.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Jell-O rolls out line with no artificial dyes, less sugar as parents seek healthier snacks
headlines.description: Jell-O joins other major snack brands in cutting out artificial dyes and sugar in its new “Simply Jell-O" products, in line with a push from some parents and the White House urging food makers to stop using fake dyes. NBC News’ Vicky Nguyen reports.

headlines.title: How to prepare yourself or a loved one to truly live a longer life
headlines.description: The average American born in the year 2024 is expected to live to age 79. Joe Coughlin, director of the MIT AgeLab, suggests you can start by asking yourself some simple questions.

headlines.title: Americans exposed to Hantavirus upset about being forced to quarantine in Nebraska
headlines.description: An American quarantining in Nebraska is speaking out to NBC News, saying he wants to continue his isolation at home but is not being allowed to do that, despite initially being told the quarantine would be voluntary. This as an American battling another life-threatening disease, ebola, is transported to Germany for care. NBC News’ Maggie Vespa has the story.

headlines.title: Rob Lowe wants to encourage cancer patients to sign up for cancer trials
headlines.description: Doctors across the U.S. are raising alarms: not enough cancer patients are enrolling in clinical trials and it’s putting the future of treatment at risk. That is why Serene Hesri, a cancer patient now in remission, and actor Rob Lowe want to raise awareness for people who think they have run out of cancer treatment options. NBC News' Akshay Syal has this story.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.
